source.access_method: manual login by licensed agent; export driven via Playwright (compliant)

data_quality_notes: pending_sales for May 2026 is intentionally set to null in current_month so the website note falls back to active listings. The raw May new-pending count (36) is depressed by late MLS status posting on a not-yet-fully-settled month: pendings fell double digits in all four South County markets at once, which is the signature of late posting rather than a real demand drop. Do not feature the May pending number until it settles. The year_over_year_may block preserves the raw figure (36 vs 51) for the record.

Median sales price for May 2026 ($2,237,000) is consistent with April 2026 ($2,212,500) but still runs above the multi-month base; SFR monthly medians are volatile at low transaction counts. The durable signal is the supply squeeze plus fast DOM, not the headline price. Lean on the ~10% year-over-year framing.

All metrics sourced from the 2026-06-16 market-moves InfoSparks pull (data/market_stats/2026-05_market_moves.json), San Clemente, Residential / Single Family, monthly. Last populated CSV row is May 2026; June is not closed yet.
market_summary: San Clemente single-family remains supply-constrained. Active listings (97) are down 37% year over year and months of supply tightened from 3.5 to 2.7, while well-priced homes still close fast (8 median days on market, down from 14 a year ago). The May median sales price of $2.24M is consistent with April and best read against the last few months rather than as a step-change. May flow metrics (pendings) are soft due to late posting and should not be featured this month.
